messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Seminar Series - Dec 2: Deep-learning Gene Perturbation Effect Prediction
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Seminar Series - Dec 2: Deep-learning Gene Perturbation Effect Prediction
messages.body_plain:
Reminder that this seminar is on today at 9 PM CET / 3 PM EST / 12 PM PST

Join Here: https://dfci.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_0nAabJt1Qeyd8QsJjvEzOQ
Paper: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12328236/


From: Maria.Doyle <Maria.Doyle at ul.ie>
Date: Friday, 14 November 2025 at 18:48
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: Bioconductor Seminar Series - Dec 2: Deep-learning Gene Perturbation Effect Prediction

Dear Bioconductor developers,

Join us for the first session in the new Bioconductor Seminar Series, designed to connect cutting-edge research with Bioconductor development.

Topic:
Deep-learning-based Gene Perturbation Effect Prediction Does Not Yet Outperform Simple Linear Baselines (Nature Methods, 2025)

Date & Time:
December 2, 2025
9 PM CET / 3 PM EST / 12 PM PST

Speakers:

  *
Constantin Ahlmann-Eltze (University College London)
  *
Wolfgang Huber (EMBL)
  *
Simon Anders (University of Heidelberg)

Discussant:
Davide Risso (University of Padova)

Format:
30 min insights ? 15 min guided discussion ? 15 min open Q&A

Register: https://dfci.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_0nAabJt1Qeyd8QsJjvEzOQ
Paper: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12328236/


Maria Doyle, PhD

Bioconductor Community Manager



School of Medicine,

University of Limerick, Limerick, V94 T9PX
Ireland

Email: maria.doyle at ul.ie<mailto:maria.doyle at ul.ie>
Phone (office): +353 61 234 768

Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *   BioCAsia 2025 (November 27-28, Adelaide, Australia)<https://biocasia2025.bioconductor.org/>
  *
EuroBioC2026 (June 3-5, Turku, Finland)<https://eurobioc2026.bioconductor.org/>
  *
BioC2026 (August 10-12, Seattle, USA)<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>



I work flexible hours across several time zones. I don't expect you to read or respond to my emails outside of your normal working hours.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Support Site account locked — Cannot reset password or add Watched Tags
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Support Site account locked — Cannot reset password or add Watched Tags
messages.body_plain:
Hi Bioconductor Team,

I am the maintainer of the package ?CrcBiomeScreen?.

I am currently unable to log in to the Support Site with my maintainer email.
When I try to log in, I get ?Invalid password?, and when I try to reset the
password, I do not receive any password reset email.

I suspect that my Support Site account is stuck due to a previous GitHub OAuth
login, so my maintainer email cannot be activated properly.

Could you please:
1. unlink or remove the GitHub OAuth?based account associated with my email,
2. activate or reset the Support Site account for my maintainer email, and
3. ensure that I can access the ?Watched Tags? field to add ?crcbiomescreen?.

Maintainer email: ngzh5554 at leeds.ac.uk

Thank you very much for your help!

Many thanks,
Chengxin

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

Subject: [Bioc-devel] Support Site account locked ? Cannot reset password or add Watched Tags

Hi Bioconductor Team,

I am the maintainer of the package ?CrcBiomeScreen?.

I am currently unable to log in to the Support Site with my maintainer email.
When I try to log in, I get ?Invalid password?, and when I try to reset the
password, I do not receive any password reset email.

I suspect that my Support Site account is stuck due to a previous GitHub OAuth
login, so my maintainer email cannot be activated properly.

Could you please:
1. unlink or remove the GitHub OAuth?based account associated with my email,
2. activate or reset the Support Site account for my maintainer email, and
3. ensure that I can access the ?Watched Tags? field to add ?crcbiomescreen?.

Maintainer email: ngzh5554 at leeds.ac.uk

Thank you very much for your help!

Many thanks,
Chengxin

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Update maintainer email for the package PostChicago
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Update maintainer email for the package PostChicago
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor team,

I am the maintainer of the package PostChicago.

I originally submitted the package with the maintainer email angelika.feldmann at dkfz-heidelberg.de, but my GitHub account is registered under a different email address.

I have now updated my GitHub account to use the email angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de, and the DESCRIPTION file in my GitHub repository also uses angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de.

Could you please update the maintainer email in your system to angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de so that I can obtain push access to the Bioconductor git repository?

Package: PostChicago
Old email: angelika.feldmann at dkfz-heidelberg.de
New email: angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de
GitHub username: FeldmannLabDKFZ

Thank you very much.

Angelika Feldmann, PhD
Mechanisms of Genome Control / B460
Junior Group Leader 

German Cancer Research Center (DKFZ)
Foundation under Public Law
Im Neuenheimer Feld 581
69120 Heidelberg
Germany
phone: +49 6221 42-1560 or +49 6221 42-3474

angelika.feldmann at dkfz-heidelberg.de 
www.dkfz.de



Management Board: Prof. Dr. med. Dr. h. c. Michael Baumann, Ursula Weyrich
VAT-ID No.: DE143293537

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] uncoverappLib repository update
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Team,

I am writing regarding the uncoverappLib package.

Due to an issue with my GitHub account (loss of access keys), I had to recreate the repository where the development version of uncoverappLib was hosted. As a consequence, the current GitHub repository history does not fully reflect the original development timeline.

In the meantime, I have made important updates to the package, including (v.2.1) (https://github.com/emanuela04/uncoverappLib/tree/v2.1.0):

  *   bug fixes in hg38
  *   improvements to existing functionality and the speed of library
  *   general maintenance updates

I would like to kindly ask for guidance on how to proceed with integrating these updates into the next Bioconductor release, and whether there are any recommended steps for handling the repository transition and package assembly under these circumstances.

Thank you very much for your time and support.

Best regards,
Emanuela Iovino, PhD

Computational Genomics Unit - BOSCO
IRCCS Azienda Ospedaliero-Universitaria di Bologna
Policlinico di Sant'Orsola, Pav. 1, Via Pelagio Palagi 9, 40138 Bologna
tel: +390512142893
email: emanuela.iovino at unibo.it<mailto:emanuela.iovino at unibo.it>; emanuela.iovino at aosp.bo.it<mailto:emanuela.iovino at aosp.bo.it>









	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Opportunity for Pre-conference Workshops - EuroBioC2026
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,

EuroBioC2026 will host on-site pre-conference workshops on June 1?2, 2026, in Turku, Finland, ahead of the main conference.

If you?re interested in hosting a workshop, please submit your interest here:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScYOBMs0JsmoZY21mCSnWSBObm-OX7NtZi6NEjY9zNdhsqL5w/viewform

? Deadline: 30 January 2026
? Decisions announced: February 2026

Selections will be based on relevance and alignment with the workshop and conference themes.

? Information on abstract submissions for short talks, posters, and workshops during the conference is available here:
https://eurobioc2026.bioconductor.org/pages/submissions.html


For any questions, contact eurobioc at bioconductor.org

Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)

From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Emanuela Iovino <emanuela.iovino at unibo.it>
Sent: Saturday, December 13, 2025 4:07 PM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>; bioc-devel-request at r-project.org <bioc-devel-request at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] uncoverappLib repository update

Dear Bioconductor Team,

I am writing regarding the uncoverappLib package.

Due to an issue with my GitHub account (loss of access keys), I had to recreate the repository where the development version of uncoverappLib was hosted. As a consequence, the current GitHub repository history does not fully reflect the original development timeline.

In the meantime, I have made important updates to the package, including (v.2.1) (https://secure-web.cisco.com/1-U0OToUty0B-4e_W4mHNOpNkIPb4gMbY2HXiv05XnA9OhG8VclQ1zw28DN1fh5XzjagJQ9tk11pUQrPFOYeRxbKC6IRKtHgM_7S5u4bil_s8uNlCU7Ym9EaCa_VdJD-GDf3Xvx2dI6lU8s_zRXYbM7lgyC3QGwzfU_zXz6kjqtvWVN7MlRUVLacK9uY4mVUUjurC8RspLrARqf0sSwQR6nv9yu61Iw1IUg1nRpGrGyEKZKZjElkf3pZFrZYvSvVcvHA1MQLKtMhMi5HOn19T0yMLrh1C89EdcU6TT18GAwUJQMp5ko6V5vkrxFaXFgDF/https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Femanuela04%2FuncoverappLib%2Ftree%2Fv2.1.0):

  *   bug fixes in hg38
  *   improvements to existing functionality and the speed of library
  *   general maintenance updates

I would like to kindly ask for guidance on how to proceed with integrating these updates into the next Bioconductor release, and whether there are any recommended steps for handling the repository transition and package assembly under these circumstances.

Thank you very much for your time and support.

Best regards,
Emanuela Iovino, PhD

Computational Genomics Unit - BOSCO
IRCCS Azienda Ospedaliero-Universitaria di Bologna
Policlinico di Sant'Orsola, Pav. 1, Via Pelagio Palagi 9, 40138 Bologna
tel: +390512142893
email: emanuela.iovino at unibo.it<mailto:emanuela.iovino at unibo.it>; emanuela.iovino at aosp.bo.it<mailto:emanuela.iovino at aosp.bo.it>

I am the maintainer of the package PostChicago.

I originally submitted the package with the maintainer email angelika.feldmann at dkfz-heidelberg.de, but my GitHub account is registered under a different email address.

I have now updated my GitHub account to use the email angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de, and the DESCRIPTION file in my GitHub repository also uses angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de.

Could you please update the maintainer email in your system to angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de so that I can obtain push access to the Bioconductor git repository?

Package: PostChicago
Old email: angelika.feldmann at dkfz-heidelberg.de
New email: angelika.feldmann at dkfz.de
GitHub username: FeldmannLabDKFZ

Thank you very much.

Angelika Feldmann, PhD

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] panoramic package on bioconductor
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I am trying to push changes to the bioconductor.git version of the panoramic package.

I am trying to follow the instructions here: https://contributions.bioconductor.org/git-version-control.html#new-package-workflow

But I am unable to create accounts at this location:
https://git.bioconductor.org/BiocCredentials/account_activation/

I am getting an error that my email (jachang4 at stanford.edu) is not associated with a maintainer of a package. I have double checked that the DESCRIPTION file of the package contains the correct email.

Can anyone assist to let me know how to proceed?

Thanks in advance,
Jake Chang



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
